component_identification.content: Standard Coup includes 15 character cards (3 each of Duke, Assassin, Captain, Ambassador, Contessa), 6 summary/reference cards, and 50 coin tokens (commonly 1-value tokens). Each player starts with 2 influence cards face down and 2 coins. Character deck composition is public knowledge even though individual cards are hidden.

core_game_loop.content: Turn rhythm: active side reveals or frames information, teammates/opponents respond under that game’s communication limits, then resolve points or survival state and rotate responsibility. Teach this as a repeating three-step cycle: present signal, interpret signal, then resolve consequences. New players should always know whose decision matters right now and what hidden information cannot be shared. Most confusion comes from breaking communication restrictions or skipping reveal order, so keep sequence strict.

detailed_rules.content: Each player starts with 2 coins and 2 hidden influence cards. On turn choose one action: Income (+1), Foreign Aid (+2, blockable by Duke), Tax as Duke (+3), Assassinate as Assassin (pay 3, target loses influence, blockable by Contessa), Steal as Captain (take up to 2 from target, blockable by Captain/Ambassador), Exchange as Ambassador (draw 2, choose cards to keep), or Coup (pay 7, unavoidable target loss). Any claim may be challenged. If challenger is wrong, challenger loses one influence; if claimant lied, claimant loses one influence and action fails (unless rules say partially resolved). Revealed influence is out of game; when you lose both cards you are eliminated. Mandatory coup at 10+ coins. Order of operations matters: declare action, allow challenge/block windows, then resolve. Eliminated players no longer act or challenge.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Win condition only: be last player with at least one unrevealed influence. Coins are a tempo resource, not points. End immediately when one player remains alive. No tiebreak exists because eliminations are sequential and definitive.

beginner_strategy.content: 1) Early game, claim simple economy actions to build coins without drawing challenge heat. 2) Challenge based on deck likelihood and table behavior, not vibes alone. 3) Hold 7+ coins pressure to force opponents into defensive lines. 4) Mix truthful and false claims so your pattern stays unreadable. 5) Track revealed cards publicly; dead copies sharply change bluff probabilities.

metadata.notes: Check 4 failed: total_token_count is 445; must be 800-3000 (hard reject if <800 or >5000)
